Headline: Pregnancy Category D
Warning Description: Do not use tetracycline during pregnancy. Tetracyclines readily cross the placenta, are found in fetal tissues and can have toxic effects on the developing fetus (retardation of skeletal development). Evidence of embryotoxicity has also been noted in animals treated early in pregnancy.

Headline: Lactation
Warning Description: Tetracyclines are excreted in breast milk. A dosage of 2 g/day for 3 days has achieved a milk plasma ratio of 0.6 to 0.8. Because of potential adverse reactions, decide whether to discontinue nursing or the drug, taking into account the importance of the drug to the mother.

Headline: Hypersensitivity
Warning Description: Hypersensitivity to any of the tetracyclines.

Headline: Pediatric Use
Warning Description: Do not use in children aged 8 years and below because of the effect of tetracyclines in teeth (permanent discoloration and possibly enamel hypoplasia) and bone.

Headline: Expired Products
Warning Description: Do not administer expired tetracyclines under any circumstance. Its degradation products are highly nephrotoxic and have on occasion, produced a Fanconi-like syndrome.

Headline: Tooth Development Discoloration Risk
Warning Description: The use of drugs of the tetracycline class during tooth development (last half of pregnancy, infancy and childhood to the age of 8 years) may cause permanent discoloration of the teeth (yellow-gray-brown).

Headline: Pediatric Use
Warning Description: Use doxycycline in pediatric patients 8 years of age or less only when the potential benefits are expected to outweigh the risks in severe or life-threatening conditions (e.g., anthrax, Rocky Mountain spotted fever), particularly when there are no alternative therapies.

Headline: Clostridium Difficile Associated Diarrhea (CDAD)
Warning Description: Clostridium difficile associated diarrhea (CDAD) has been reported with use of antibacterial agents, including doxycycline, and may range from mild diarrhea to fatal colitis. Treatment alters normal colon flora leading to overgrowth of C. difficile, which produces toxins contributing to CDAD. Hypertoxin-producing strains increase morbidity and mortality and may require colectomy. CDAD should be considered in patients with diarrhea following antibacterial use and may occur up to two months after administration. If suspected or confirmed, discontinue non-targeted antibacterial drugs and initiate appropriate management including fluids, electrolyte support, antibacterial treatment, and surgical evaluation as clinically indicated.

Headline: Severe Skin Reactions
Warning Description: Severe skin reactions, such as exfoliative dermatitis, erythema multiforme, Stevens-Johnson syndrome, toxic epidermal necrolysis, and drug reaction with eosinophilia and systemic symptoms (DRESS) have been reported in patients receiving doxycycline.

Headline: Intracranial Hypertension (IH)
Warning Description: Intracranial hypertension (IH, pseudotumor cerebri) has been associated with tetracyclines including doxycycline. Symptoms include headache, blurred vision, diplopia, vision loss, and papilledema. Women of childbearing age who are overweight or have a history of IH are at greater risk. Concomitant use with isotretinoin should be avoided. Although IH usually resolves after discontinuation, permanent visual loss may occur. If visual disturbance develops, prompt ophthalmologic evaluation is required, and patients should be monitored as intracranial pressure may remain elevated after drug cessation.

Headline: Fetal Toxicity Risk
Warning Description: Results of animal studies indicate that tetracyclines cross the placenta, are found in fetal tissues, and can have toxic effects on the developing fetus (often related to retardation of skeletal development). Evidence of embryotoxicity has also been noted in animals treated early in pregnancy. If any tetracycline is used during pregnancy or if the patient becomes pregnant while taking this drug, the patient should be apprised of the potential hazard to the fetus.

Headline: Sulfite Sensitivity Reactions
Warning Description: This contains sodium metabisulfite, a sulfite that may cause allergic-type reactions, including anaphylactic symptoms and life-threatening or less severe asthmatic episodes in certain susceptible people. The overall prevalence of sulfite sensitivity in the general population is unknown and probably low. Sulfite sensitivity is seen more frequently in asthmatic than in non-asthmatic people.

Headline: Overdosage
Warning Description: In case of overdosage, discontinue medication, treat symptomatically and institute supportive measures. Dialysis does not alter serum half-life and thus would not be of benefit in treating cases of overdosage.

Headline: Hypersensitivity
Warning Description: This drug is contraindicated in persons who have shown hypersensitivity to any of the tetracyclines.

Headline: Photosensitivity
Warning Description: Photosensitivity manifested by an exaggerated sunburn reaction has been observed in some individuals taking tetracycline. Patients apt to be exposed to direct sunlight or ultraviolet light should be advised that this reaction can occur with tetracycline drugs and treatment should be discontinued at the first evidence of skin erythema.

Headline: Bone Growth and Calcium Complex Formation
Warning Description: As with other tetracyclines, this drug forms a stable calcium complex in any bone-forming tissue. A decrease in the fibula growth rate has been observed in prematures given oral Tetracycline in dose 25 mg/kg every 6 hours. This reaction was shown to be reversible when the drug was discontinued.

Headline: Increase in BUN
Warning Description: The anti-anabolic action of the tetracycline may cause an increase in BUN, Doxycycline rarely gives rise to increased urea concentration in renal failure.

Headline: Overdosage
Warning Description: In case of overdosage, discontinue medication. Treat symptomatically and institute supportive measures. Dialysis doses not alter serum half-life and thus would not be of benefit in treating cases of overdosage.

Headline: Hypersensitivity to Tetracyclines
Warning Description: The drug is contraindicated in patients who have shown hypersensitivity to any of the tetracyclines.

Headline: Tooth Discoloration and Enamel Hypoplasia
Warning Description: The use of the drugs of the tetracycline class in tooth development (last half of pregnancy, infancy and childhood to the age of 8 years) may cause permanent discoloration of the teeth (yellow-gray-brown). This adverse reaction is more common during long term use of the drugs but has been observed following repeated short-term courses. Enamel hypoplasia has also been reported. Tetracycline drugs, therefore, should not be used in this age group unless other drugs are not likely to be effective or as contraindicated.

Headline: Overgrowth of Non-susceptible Organisms
Warning Description: As with other antibiotics, use of this drug may result in overgrowth of non-susceptible organisms, including fungi. If super infection occurs, appropriate therapy should be instituted.

Headline: Pediatric Use
Warning Description: Use in Children: The drug should not be used in children below 8 years old.

Headline: Pregnancy Category D
Warning Description: Do not use tetracycline during pregnancy. Tetracyclines readily cross the placenta, are found in fetal tissues and can have toxic effects on the developing fetus (retardation of skeletal development). Evidence of embryotoxicity has also been noted in animals treated early in pregnancy.

Headline: Lactation Risk
Warning Description: Tetracyclines are excreted in human milk. Consider the risk of potential serious adverse effects to the infant during breastfeeding.

Headline: Overdosage
Warning Description: Clinical features may include CNS symptoms (confusion, dizziness, impaired consciousness, convulsions), QT interval prolongation, and GI effects (nausea, mucosal erosions). Post-marketing reports include hallucination and tremor. Management is symptomatic with observation, hydration, and ECG monitoring for QT prolongation. Activated charcoal may reduce absorption if given early. Antacids may protect gastric mucosa. Hemodialysis, peritoneal dialysis, and CAPD are ineffective. No specific antidote exists.

Headline: Serious Adverse Reactions (Tendinitis, Neuropathy, CNS Effects)
Warning Description: Includes tendinitis, tendon rupture, peripheral neuropathy, CNS effects, and exacerbation of myasthenia gravis. These reactions may be disabling and potentially irreversible. Discontinue levofloxacin immediately if they occur and avoid fluoroquinolones in affected patients. May worsen muscle weakness in myasthenia gravis.

Headline: Hypersensitivity
Warning Description: Known hypersensitivity to levofloxacin, other quinolones or to any component of the product. Patients with epilepsy. Patients with history of tendinitis or tendon rupture associated with the use of any member of the quinolone group of antimicrobial agents.

Headline: Restricted Use in Uncomplicated Infections
Warning Description: Because fluoroquinolones, including levofloxacin have been associated with serious adverse reactions, reserve levofloxacin for use in patients who have no alternative treatment options for the following indications: Uncomplicated urinary tract infection; Acute bacterial exacerbation of chronic bronchitis; Acute bacterial sinusitis.

Headline: Pregnancy Category C
Warning Description: There are no adequate and well controlled studies to date using levofloxacin in pregnant women. The drug should not be used in pregnant women unless the potential benefits justify the potential risks to the fetus.

Headline: Lactation Risk
Warning Description: Levofloxacin has not been measured in human milk. However, based on the ofloxacin data, it can be presumed that levofloxacin will be excreted in human milk. Therefore, a decision should be made whether to discontinue breastfeeding or to discontinue the drug, taking into account the importance of the drug to the mother as well as the possible serious adverse effects to the infant.

Headline: Overdosage
Warning Description: Levofloxacin exhibits a low potential for acute toxicity. In the event of an acute overdosage, the stomach should be emptied. The patient should be observed and appropriate hydration maintained. Levofloxacin is not efficiently removed by hemodialysis or peritoneal dialysis.

Headline: Hypersensitivity
Warning Description: Levofloxacin is contraindicated in patients with a history or known hypersensitivity to this drug and/or other fluoroquinolone antibacterials.

Headline: Pediatric Use
Warning Description: Levofloxacin is not indicated for pediatric patients (less than 18 years of age). An increased incidence of musculoskeletal disorders (arthralgia, arthritis, tendonopathy, and gait abnormality) has been observed in these patients.

Headline: Epilepsy and Tendon Disorder History
Warning Description: Patients with epilepsy; history of tendon disorders related to fluoroquinolone administration.

Headline: Use in Pregnancy
Warning Description: Levofloxacin should be used during pregnancy only if the potential benefit justifies the potential risk to the fetus.

Headline: Use in Pregnancy
Warning Description: There are no adequate and well-controlled studies in pregnant women. The safety and efficacy in pregnant women has not been established.

Headline: Use in Lactation
Warning Description: Levofloxacin has not been measured in human milk. It can be presumed to be excreted in human milk. Nursing should not be undertaken due to potential serious adverse reactions in nursing infants.

Headline: Overdosage
Warning Description: Levofloxacin tablets do not seem to be especially toxic in overdose. Treatment varies depending on the case. Recent overdose may require medicines or gastric “pump the stomach.” Supportive care includes IV fluids if necessary and monitoring of vital signs such as heart rate and breathing. Other treatments depend on complications. Seek medical attention immediately.

Headline: Tendon Effects
Warning Description: Tendon rupture involving shoulder, hand, or Achilles tendon has been reported. Risk is higher in patients receiving corticosteroids and in the elderly. Discontinue levofloxacin if pain, inflammation, or rupture occurs. Patients should rest and avoid exercise until tendon disorders are excluded. Rupture can occur during or after therapy.

Headline: Peripheral Neuropathy
Warning Description: Peripheral neuropathies have been associated with levofloxacin use. Symptoms include pain, burning, tingling, numbness, or weakness. Patients should discontinue treatment if symptoms develop. Contact physician immediately upon symptom onset. Condition may occur during therapy.

Headline: QT Prolongation
Warning Description: Levofloxacin has been associated with QT interval prolongation and arrhythmia. Rare cases of torsades de pointes have been reported. Avoid use in patients with known QT prolongation or uncorrected hypokalemia. Avoid with class IA or class III antiarrhythmics. Monitoring is advised in at-risk patients.

Headline: Elderly QT Risk
Warning Description: Elderly patients may be more susceptible to QT interval effects. Use caution with drugs that prolong QT interval. Risk increases with factors like hypokalemia or known QT prolongation. Concomitant antiarrhythmics increase risk. Monitor closely in elderly patients.

Headline: Renal Function
Warning Description: Levofloxacin is substantially excreted by the kidney. Risk of toxicity is higher in patients with impaired renal function. Elderly patients are more likely to have decreased renal function. Dose selection should be done carefully. Monitoring of renal function is advisable.

Headline: Hypersensitivity
Warning Description: Sterile Cefotaxime Sodium is contraindicated to patients with known hypersensitivity to cephalosporins.

Headline: Use in Lactation
Warning Description: Although cefatoxime is excreted in breast milk in small amounts, no adverse effects have been observed in breast-fed infants whose mothers were receiving cefatoxime, and the American Academy of Pediatrics considers that it is therefore usually compatible with breastfeeding.

Headline: Overdosage
Warning Description: Similar effects noted on regular doses. Adverse events experienced in higher than recommended dose were similar effects noted on regular doses. The typical symptoms of an overdose include reversible loss of hearing, severe nausea, vomiting and diarrhea.

Headline: Hypersensitivity
Warning Description: Azithromycin is contraindicated in patients with known hypersensitivity to azithromycin, erythromycin, any macrolide or ketolide antibiotic.

Headline: Pregnancy Category B
Warning Description: Reproduction studies have been performed in rats and mice at doses up to moderately maternally toxic dose concentrations (i.e., 200 mg/kg/day). These doses, based on a mg/m2 basis, are estimated to be 4 and 2 times, respectively, the human daily dose of 500 mg. In the animal studies, no evidence of harm to the fetus due to azithromycin was found. There are, however, no adequate and well-controlled studies in pregnant women. Because animal reproduction studies are not always predictive of human response, azithromycin should be used during pregnancy only if clearly needed.

Headline: Use in Lactation
Warning Description: Nursing Mothers: It is not known whether azithromycin is excreted in human milk. Because many drugs are excreted in human milk, caution should be exercised when azithromycin is administered to a nursing woman.

Headline: Overdosage
Warning Description: Adverse events experienced in higher than recommended doses were similar to those seen at normal doses. The typical symptoms of an overdose with macrolide antibiotics include reversible loss of hearing, severe nausea, vomiting and diarrhoea. In the event of overdose, the administration of medicinal charcoal and general symptomatic treatment and supportive measures are indicated as required.

Headline: Hypersensitivity
Warning Description: Hypersensitivity to the active substance, erythromycin, any macrolide or ketolide antibiotic, or to any of the excipients.

Headline: Use In Pregnancy
Warning Description: There are no adequate data from the use of azithromycin in pregnant women. In reproduction toxicity studies in animals, azithromycin was shown to pass the placenta, but no teratogenic effects were observed. The safety of azithromycin has not been confirmed with regard to the use of the active substance during pregnancy. Therefore, azithromycin should only be used during pregnancy if the benefit outweighs the risk.

Headline: Use in Lactation
Warning Description: Azithromycin has been reported to be secreted into human breast milk, but there are no adequate and well-controlled clinical studies in breastfeeding women that have characterised the pharmacokinetics of azithromycin excretion into human breast milk.

Headline: Fertility
Warning Description: In fertility studies conducted in rat, reduced pregnancy rates were noted following administration of azithromycin. The relevance of this finding to humans is unknown.

Headline: Overdosage
Warning Description: Do not exceed the recommended doses. In case of overdosage consult the physician immediately.

Headline: Hypersensitivity
Warning Description: Hypersensitivity to azithromycin or any of the macrolide antibiotics. Co-administration with ergot derivatives.

Headline: Use In Pregnancy
Warning Description: Azithromycin should only be used during pregnancy if it is clearly indicated. Caution should be taken when administered to a pregnant woman.

Headline: Use in Lactation
Warning Description: It is not known whether azithromycin is excreted in human milk. Caution should be taken when administered to a nursing woman.

Headline: Overdosage
Warning Description: Gastrointestinal effects eg, nausea, vomiting and diarrhea may be evident, and symptoms of water/electrolyte imbalance should be treated symptomatically. Amoxicillin crystalluria, in some cases leading to renal failure, has been observed. Amoxil can be removed from the circulation by hemodialysis.

Headline: Hypersensitivity
Warning Description: Amoxicillin (Amoxil) is a penicillin and should not be given to patients w/ history of hypersensitivity to ß-lactam antibiotics (eg, penicillins, cephalosporins).

Headline: Use in Pregnancy
Warning Description: The safety of Amoxil/Amoxil Forte for use in human pregnancy has not been established by well-controlled studies in pregnant women. Reproduction studies have been performed in mice and rats at doses up to 10 times the human dose and these studies have revealed no evidence of impaired fertility or harm to the fetus due to amoxicillin.

Headline: Use in Pregnancy
Warning Description: Amoxil may be used in pregnancy when the potential benefits outweigh the potential risks associated with treatment.

Headline: Use in Lactation
Warning Description: Amoxil may be given during lactation. With the exception of the risk of sensitization associated with the excretion of trace quantities of amoxicillin in breast milk, there are no known detrimental effects for the breastfed infant.

Headline: Overdosage
Warning Description: Amoxicillin is of low toxicity, although nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, and allergic manifestations may occur. Symptomatic treatment is required for almost all patients. Problems are unlikely if adequate fluid intake and urinary output are maintained; however, crystalluria and hemorrhagic cystitis are possible.

Headline: Hypersensitivity
Warning Description: Hypersensitivity to penicillins and to any ingredient in the products.

Headline: Infectious Mononucleosis
Warning Description: Patients with infectious mononucleosis due to possible development of rash during therapy.

Headline: Use in Pregnancy
Warning Description: Amoxicillin has been administered to pregnant women without evidence of adverse effects to the fetus. The use of amoxicillin is currently included in the US Center for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) recommendations for the treatment of chlamydial infections during pregnancy and for the treatment of cutaneous anthrax or for post-exposure prophylaxis after exposure to Bacillus anthracis spores.

Headline: Use in Lactation
Warning Description: Use with caution in breastfeeding mothers because amoxicillin is distributed into milk and may lead to sensitization of infants.

Headline: Overdosage
Warning Description: Clinical features of amoxicillin overdosage may include allergic reactions and GI effects such as nausea, vomiting and diarrhea. Symptomatic treatment with attention to water/electrolyte balance is recommended.

Headline: Overdosage
Warning Description: Crystalluria, in some cases leading to renal failure, has been observed after amoxicillin overdosage in adults and children. Adequate fluid intake and diuresis should be maintained to reduce the risk of amoxicillin crystalluria.

Headline: Overdosage
Warning Description: Interstitial nephritis resulting in oliguric renal failure has also been reported in a small number of patients after overdose with amoxicillin. Renal impairment appears to be reversible with cessation of drug administration. High blood levels may occur more readily in patients with impaired renal function because of decreased renal clearance of amoxicillin.

Headline: Overdosage
Warning Description: Amoxicillin may be removed from circulation by hemodialysis.

Headline: Hypersensitivity
Warning Description: Known hypersensitivity to penicillins or cephalosporins, or any component of the product.

Headline: Patient with Infectious Mononucleosis
Warning Description: Patient with infectious mononucleosis since amoxicillin is likely to cause a maculopapular rash.

Headline: Pregnancy Category B
Warning Description: The safety of amoxicillin during pregnancy has not been definitely established. However, amoxicillin has been administered to pregnant women, particularly in the treatment of urinary tract infections, without evidence of adverse effects to the fetus.

Headline: Use in Lactation
Warning Description: Since amoxicillin is distributed into human milk and may lead to sensitization of the infant, the drug should be used with caution when breastfeeding.

Headline: Overdosage
Warning Description: Overdosage of Clarithromycin can cause gastrointestinal symptoms such as abdominal pain, vomiting, nausea, and diarrhea.

Headline: Overdosage
Warning Description: Adverse reactions accompanying overdosage should be treated by the prompt elimination of unabsorbed drug and supportive measures.

Headline: Overdosage
Warning Description: As with other macrolides, Clarithromycin serum concentrations are not expected to be appreciably affected by hemodialysis or peritoneal dialysis.

Headline: Hypersensitivity
Warning Description: Clarithromycin is contraindicated in patients with known hypersensitivity to any macrolide antibiotics.

Headline: Contraindications
Warning Description: Astemizole, cisapride and terfenadine are contraindicated in patients on Clarithromycin.

Headline: Use in Pregnancy
Warning Description: Clarithromycin should not be used in pregnant women except in clinical circumstances where no alternative therapy is appropriate.

Headline: Use in Pregnancy
Warning Description: If pregnancy occurs while taking this drug, the patient should be apprised of the potential hazard to the fetus.

Headline: Use in Lactation
Warning Description: It is not known whether Clarithromycin is excreted in breast milk.

Headline: Use in Lactation
Warning Description: Exercise caution when administering to a nursing woman.

Headline: Use in Pregnancy
Warning Description: The safety of clarithromycin for use during pregnancy has not been established. Based on variable results obtained from studies in mice, rats, rabbits and monkeys, the possibility of adverse effects on embryo-foetal development cannot be excluded. Therefore, use during pregnancy is not advised without carefully weighing the benefits against risk.

Headline: Breastfeeding
Warning Description: The safety of clarithromycin for using during breast-feeding of infants has not been established. Clarithromycin is excreted into human breast milk.

Headline: Gastro-intestinal Symptoms from Overdose
Warning Description: Reports indicate that the ingestion of large amounts of clarithromycin can be expected to produce gastro-intestinal symptoms.

Headline: CNS and Metabolic Effects from Overdose
Warning Description: One patient who had a history of bipolar disorder ingested 8 grams of clarithromycin and showed altered mental status, paranoid behaviour, hypokalemia and hypoxemia.

Headline: Management Overdose
Warning Description: Adverse reactions accompanying overdose should be treated by the prompt elimination of unabsorbed drug and supportive measures.

Headline: Dialysis Effect on Clarithromycin Levels
Warning Description: As with other macrolides, Clarithromycin serum levels are not expected to be appreciably affected by haemodialysis or peritoneal dialysis.

Headline: Hypersensitivity to Macrolides
Warning Description: Hypersensitivity to macrolide antibiotic drugs or to any of its excipients.

Headline: Ergot Alkaloid Interaction
Warning Description: Concomitant administration of clarithromycin and ergot alkaloids (e.g. ergotamine or dihydroergotamine) is contraindicated, as this may result in ergot toxicity.

Headline: Oral Midazolam Interaction
Warning Description: Concomitant administration of clarithromycin and oral midazolam is contraindicated.

Headline: QT Prolongation Drug Interaction
Warning Description: Concomitant administration of clarithromycin and any of the following drugs is contraindicated: astemizole, cisapride, pimozide and terfenadine as this may result in QT prolongation and cardiac arrhythmias, including ventricular tachycardia, ventricular fibrillation, and torsades de pointes.

Headline: History of QT Prolongation or Arrhythmia
Warning Description: Clarithromycin should not be given to patients with history of QT prolongation (congenital or documented acquired QT prolongation) or ventricular cardiac arrhythmia, including torsades de pointes.

Headline: Ticagrelor or Ranolazine Interaction
Warning Description: Concomitant administration with ticagrelor or ranolazine is contraindicated.

Headline: GI Symptoms in Overdosage
Warning Description: Clinical features of clarithromycin overdosage may include GI symptoms such as abdominal pain, nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea.

Headline: CNS and Metabolic Effects from Overdose
Warning Description: One patient who had a history of bipolar disorder ingested 8 g of clarithromycin and showed altered mental status, paranoid behavior, hypokalemia and hypoxemia.

Headline: Management of Overdosage
Warning Description: Adverse reactions accompanying overdosage should be treated promptly by elimination of unabsorbed drug and supportive measures.

Headline: Dialysis Effect on Drug Levels
Warning Description: As with other macrolides, clarithromycin serum concentrations are not expected to be appreciably removed by hemodialysis or peritoneal dialysis.

Headline: Hypersensitivity to Macrolides
Warning Description: Known hypersensitivity to clarithromycin, erythromycin or any macrolide antibiotic, or to any component of the product.

Headline: History of Hepatic Dysfunction
Warning Description: Patients with a history of cholestatic jaundice/hepatic dysfunction associated with prior use of clarithromycin.

Headline: QT Prolongation or Arrhythmia History
Warning Description: Patients with a history of QT prolongation or ventricular cardiac arrythmia, including torsades de pointes.

Headline: QT-Prolonging Drug Interactions
Warning Description: Concomitant administration with cisapride, pimozide, astemizaole, terfenadine, and ergotamine or dihydroergotamine which may result in cardiac arrhythmias (QT prolongation, ventricular tachycardia, ventricular fibrillation, and torsades de pointes) leading to death.

Headline: Statin Interaction
Warning Description: Concomitant administration with simvastatin or lovastatin.

Headline: Ticagrelor or Ranolazine Interaction
Warning Description: Concomitant administration with ticagrelor or ranolazine.

Headline: Colchicine Interaction in Renal/Heaptic Impairment
Warning Description: Concomitant administration of clarithromycin and colchicine in patients with renal or hepatic impairment.

Headline: Severe Renal Impairment (ER Tablet)
Warning Description: Clarithromycin ER tablet is contraindicated in patients with creatinine clearance <30 mL/min.

Headline: Hypersensitivity (Suspension)
Warning Description: Clarithromycin is contraindicated in patients with a known hypersensitivity to Clarithromycin, Erythromycin or any of the macrolide antibiotics.

Headline: Contraindicated Drug Interactions (Suspension)
Warning Description: Concomitant administration of Clarithromycin with Cisapride, Pimozide, Astemizole or Terfenadine is contraindicated.

Headline: Pregnancy Risk
Warning Description: There are no adequate and well-controlled studies in pregnant women.

Headline: Risk-benefit in Pregnancy
Warning Description: The benefits against risk, particularly during the first trimester of pregnancy should be carefully weighed by a physician.

Headline: Animal Study Findings (Cleft Palate)
Warning Description: Four studies in mice revealed a variable incidence of cleft palate after oral doses of 1000 mg/kg/day during gestation days 6 to 15.

Headline: Fetal Growth Effects
Warning Description: In monkeys, an oral dose of 70 mg/kg/day produced fetal growth retardation at plasma levels 2 times the human serum levels.

Headline: No Teratogenicity in Some Studies
Warning Description: Studies in rats and rabbits failed to demonstrate any teratogenicity from clarithromycin.

Headline: Cardiovascular Anomalies in Animal Studies
Warning Description: A low incidence of cardiovascular anomalies at doses of 150 mg/kg/day administered during gestation days 6 to 15.

Headline: Embryonic Loss
Warning Description: Embryonic loss has been seen in monkeys and rabbits.

Headline: Breastfeeding Caution
Warning Description: Since clarithromycin is distributed in human milk, caution should be exercised when the drug is given to breastfeeding women.

Headline: Overdosage Management
Warning Description:
It is not described poisoning at therapeutical doses.
In the event of overdose, it is enough increase urinary elimination of drug substance by oral liquids administration.

Headline: Hypersensitivity
Warning Description: Hypersensitivity to the active substance or to any of the excipients listed in Description. Fosfomycin trometamol is contraindicated in: Patients with known hypersensitivity to the drug; Patients with severe renal insufficiency (Clcr <10 mL/min); Patients undergone haemodialysis; Children younger than 12 years old.

Headline: Pregnancy
Warning Description: Pregnancy: Several studies on animals with fosfomycin trometamol have not shown any evidence of damaging effects on the fetus. Previous studies in rats, sodium and calcium fosfomycin we could only detect toxic effects on fetus when using 25 times therapeutic dose. In rabbits the minimum dose doesn't show toxicity signs.

Headline: Pregnancy
Warning Description: In spite of results of studies on animals with fosfomycin trometamol have not shown any teratogenic effect, and single dose administration in pregnancy reduce teratogenic risk compared with longer treatments, the administration is only recommended when the benefit/ risk balance is considered favourable.

Headline: Breastfeeding
Warning Description: Breastfeeding: Fosfomycin trometamol is excreted into milk during breastfeeding, in consequence, the administration is only recommended in breastfeeding when the benefit/risk balance is considered favourable.

Headline: Treatment
Warning Description: In the event of overdose, the patient must be monitored (particularly for plasma/serum electrolyte levels), and treatment should be symptomatic and supportive.

Headline: Rehydration and Elimination
Warning Description: Rehydration is recommended to promote urinary elimination of the active substance. Fosfomycin is effectively cleared from the body by haemodialysis with a mean elimination half- life of approximately 4 hours.

Headline: Hypersensitivity
Warning Description: Hypersensitivity to the active substance Fosfomycin.

Headline: Pregnancy
Warning Description: Only limited data on the safety of Fosfomycin trometamol treatment during 1st trimester of pregnancy (n=152) are available. These data do not raise any safety signal for teratogenicity so far. Fosfomycin crosses the placenta.

Headline: Animal Studies
Warning Description: Animal studies have not indicated any direct or indirect toxicity with any effect on pregnancy, embryonic development, development of the foetus and/or postnatal development. Fosfomycin should only be used during pregnancy, if clearly necessary.

Headline: Lactation
Warning Description: Since Fosfomycin passes into breast milk even after taking just a single dose, women who are breastfeeding should not be treated with Fosfomycin unless strictly necessary.

Headline: Fertility
Warning Description: No data in humans are available. In male and female rats, oral administration of fosfomycin up to 1000 mg/kg/d did not impair fertility.

Headline: Hypersensitivity
Warning Description: Do not use in patients with a known allergy to Fosfomycin.

Headline: Severe Renal Failure & Patients on Hemodialysis
Warning Description: Do not use in patients with severe renal failure & patients on hemodialysis.

Headline: Hypersensitivity
Warning Description: Do not use in patients with a known allergy to Sulfamethoxazole.

Headline: Use in Pregnancy and Lactation
Warning Description: No adverse effects have been seen in breast-fed infants whose mothers were taking cotrimoxazole, and the last available guidance from the American Academy of Pediatrics considered that it was therefore usually compatible with breast feeding.

Headline: Sodium Metabisulfite Allergic Reactions
Warning Description: Sodium metabisulfite contains a sulfite that may cause allergic type reactions, including anaphylactic symptoms and life threatening or less severe asthmatic episode in certain susceptible persons.

Headline: Overdosage of Cephalosporins
Warning Description: Overdosage of cephalosporins can cause cerebral irritation leading to convulsions or encephalopathy. Serum levels of cefuroxime can be reduced by hemodialysis and peritoneal dialysis.

Headline: Cephalosporin Allergy
Warning Description: Cefuroxime (as Axetil) products are contraindicated in patients with known allergy to the cephalosporin group of antibiotics.

Headline: Previous Hypersensitivity Reactions
Warning Description: Before therapy with Cefuroxime (as Axetil) products is instituted, careful inquiry should be made to determine whether the patient has had previous hypersensitivity reactions to Cefuroxime (as Axetil) products, other cephalosporins, penicillins, or other drugs.

Headline: Penicillin-sensitive Patients
Warning Description: If this product is to be given to penicillin-sensitive patients, caution should be exercised because cross-hypersensitivity among beta-lactam antibiotics has been clearly documented and may occur in up to 10% of patients with a history of penicillin allergy.

Headline: Serious Acute Hypersensitivity Reactions
Warning Description: If a clinically significant allergic reaction to Cefuroxime (as Axetil) products occurs, discontinue the drug and institute appropriate therapy. Serious acute hypersensitivity reactions may require treatment with epinephrine and other emergency measures, including oxygen, intravenous fluids, intravenous antihistamines, corticosteroids, pressor amines, and airway management, as clinically indicated.

Headline: Clostridium Difficile Associated Diarrhea (CDAD)
Warning Description: Clostridium difficile associated diarrhea (CDAD) has been reported with use of nearly all antibacterial agents, including Cefuroxime (as Axetil), and may range in severity from mild diarrhea to fatal colitis.

Headline: C. Difficile Toxins and Infection Severity
Warning Description: C. difficile produces toxins A and B which contribute to the development of CDAD. Hypertoxin producing strains of C. difficile cause increased morbidity and mortality, as these infections can be refractory to antimicrobial therapy and may require colectomy.

Headline: CDAD After Antibiotic Use
Warning Description: CDAD must be considered in all patients who present with diarrhea following antibiotic use. Careful medical history is necessary since CDAD has been reported to occur over 2 months after the administration of antibacterial agents.

Headline: CDAD Management
Warning Description: If CDAD is suspected or confirmed, ongoing antibiotic use not directed against C. difficile may need to be discontinued. Appropriate fluid and electrolyte management, protein supplementation, antibiotic treatment of C. difficile, and surgical evaluation should be instituted as clinically indicated.

Headline: Pregnancy Category B
Warning Description: Reproduction studies have been performed in mice at doses up to 3,200 mg/kg/day (14 times the recommended maximum human dose based on mg/m2) and in rats at doses up to 1,000 mg/kg/day (9 times the recommended maximum human dose based on mg/m2) and have revealed no evidence of impaired fertility or harm to the fetus due to Cefuroxime (as Axetil).

Headline: Labor and Delivery Use
Warning Description: Cefuroxime (as Axetil) has not been studied for use during labor and delivery.

Headline: Lactation
Warning Description: Because cefuroxime is excreted in human milk, consideration should be given to discontinuing nursing temporarily during treatment with Cefuroxime (as Axetil).

Headline: Overdosage of Cephalosporins
Warning Description: Overdosage of cephalosporins can cause cerebral irritation leading to convulsions. Serum levels of cefuroxime can be reduced by hemodialysis and peritoneal dialysis.

Headline: Hypersensitivity to Cephalosporins
Warning Description: Cefuroxime axetil is contraindicated in patients with known allergy to the cephalosporin group of antibiotics.

Headline: Clostridium Difficile Associated Diarrhea (CDAD)
Warning Description: Clostridium difficile-associated diarrhea (CDAD) has been reported with use nearly all antibacterial agents, including cefuroxime axetil, and may range in severity from mild diarrhea to fatal colitis.

Headline: Treatment with Antibacterial Agents Alters the Normal Flora of the Colon
Warning Description: Treatment with antibacterial agents alters the normal flora of the colon leading to overgrowth of C. difficile.

Headline: Clostridium Difficile Produces Toxins A and B
Warning Description: Clostridium difficile produces toxins A and B which contribute to the development of CDAD.

Headline: Hypertoxin Producing Strains of C. Difficile
Warning Description: Hypertoxin producing strains of C. difficile cause increased morbidity and mortality, as these infections can be refractory to antimicrobial therapy and may require colectomy.

Headline: Clostridium Difficile
Warning Description: Clostridium difficile must be considered in all patients who present with diarrhea following antibiotic use. Careful medical history is necessary since CDAD has been reported to occur over 2 months after the administration of antibacterial agents.

Headline: If CDAD is Suspected or Confirmed
Warning Description: If CDAD is suspected or confirmed, ongoing antibiotic use not directed against C. difficile may need to be discontinued. Appropriate fluid and electrolyte management, protein supplementation, antibiotic treatment of C. difficile and surgical evaluation should be instituted as clinically indicated.

Headline: Hypersensitivity to Penicillin
Warning Description: Cefumax should be used with care in patients with hypersensitivity to penicillin.

Headline: Cross-allergenicity Between Penicillin and Cephalosporin
Warning Description: In the clinical and laboratory tests, cross-allergenicity between penicillin and cephalosporin was ascertained although there were rare cases of patients who have demonstrated allergic reactions to both of these 2 drugs.

Headline: Anaphylactic Shock
Warning Description: Some cases of anaphylactic shock were reported especially following the parenteral administration.

Headline: During Pregnancy and Children Below 3 Months
Warning Description: During pregnancy and children <3 months, cefumax should be used in cases of absolute necessity and under direct medical control.

Headline: Hypersensitivity to Cephalosporins
Warning Description: Cefuroxime should not be given to patients who are hypersensitive to it or to other cephalosporins.

Headline: Penicillin-sensitive Patients
Warning Description: About 10% of penicillin-sensitive patients may also be allergic to cephalosporins although the true incidence is uncertain; great care should be taken if cefalotin is to be given to such patients.

Headline: Patients with History of Allergy
Warning Description: Care is also necessary in patients with a history of allergy.

Headline: Hypersensitivity to Ciprofloxacin or Quinolone Chemotherapeutics
Warning Description: Ciprofloxacin must not be used in cases of hypersensitivity to Ciprofloxacin or other quinolone chemotherapeutics.

Headline: Children, Adolescents, Pregnant Women or Nursing Mothers
Warning Description: Ciprofloxacin must not be prescribed for children, adolescents, pregnant women or nursing mothers, since there is no experience on the drug's safety in these patient groups and since, on the basis of animal studies, it is not entirely improbable that the drug could cause damage to articular cartilage in the immature organism. Animal studies have not yet yielded any evidence of teratogenic effects (malformations).

Headline: Epileptics and Patients with CNS Disorders
Warning Description: In epileptics and in patients who have suffered from previous CNS disorders (e.g. lowered convulsion threshold, previous history of convulsion, reduced cerebral blood flow, altered brain structure or stroke), Ciprofloxacin should only be used where the benefits of treatment exceed the risks, since these patients are endangered because of possible central nervous side effects.

Headline: Antacid Preparations
Warning Description: Do not use Ciprofloxacin simultaneously with antacid preparations containing Magnesium Hydroxide or Aluminium Hydroxide. An interval of at least 2 hours must be respected between the administration of an antacid and Ciprofloxacin.

Headline: Well Hydration to Prevent Crystalluria
Warning Description: Patients receiving Ciprofloxacin must be well hydrated to prevent crystalluria.

Headline: Excessive Alkalinisation of Urine
Warning Description: Excessive alkalinisation of urine should be avoided.

Headline: Xanthine Derivatives and Theophylline Interaction
Warning Description: When Xanthine derivatives like theophylline were co-administrated with Ciprofloxacin the plasma levels of theophylline were raised to the toxic range. In this case suitable dose reduction of theophylline may be considered.

Headline: Hypersensitivity to Ciprofloxacin and other Quinolone Antibiotics
Warning Description: Hypersensitivity to ciprofloxacin and other quinolone antibiotics.

Headline: Acute Overdosage and Reversible Renal Toxicity
Warning Description: In the event of acute overdosage, reversible renal toxicity has been reported in some cases. Therefore, apart from routine emergency measures, it is recommended to monitor renal function and to administer magnesium- or calcium-containing antacids which reduce the absorption of ciprofloxacin.

Headline: Caution in Epileptic Patients and CNS Disorders
Warning Description: Caution should be taken when giving ciprofloxacin to epileptic patients and to those with history of CNS disorders, myasthenia gravis and glucose-6-phosphate dehydrogenase deficiency.

Headline: Myasthenia Gravis Exacerbation
Warning Description: Ciprofloxacin may exacerbate or unmask symptoms of myasthenia gravis.

Headline: Tendon Pain, Inflammation or Rupture
Warning Description: If tendon pain, inflammation or rupture occurs, treatment with ciprofloxacin should be discontinued.

Headline: Children, Adolescents, Pregnant Women and Lactating Mothers
Warning Description: Ciprofloxacin is contraindicated in children, adolescents, pregnant women and lactating mothers.

Headline: Arthralgia and Degenerative Joint Changes in Young Animals
Warning Description: Ciprofloxacin was reported to cause arthralgia and degenerative changes in joints that bear weight in young animals.

Headline: Acute Toxicitiy in Humans
Warning Description: Limited information is available on the acute toxicity of cefixime in humans. In healthy adults who received cefixime in single doses up to 2 g, adverse effects were similar to those seen with usual doses of the drug and included mild to moderate adverse GI effects.

Headline: Hypersensitivity to Cefixime or Cephalosporins
Warning Description: Known hypersensitivity to cefixime or other cephalosporins, to penicillins or any component of the product.

Headline: Hypersensitivity to Cefixime or Cephalosporins
Warning Description: Hypersensitivity to cefixime or other cephalosporins.

Headline: Hypersensitivity to Cefixime, Cephalosporins, or Penicillins
Warning Description: Known to hypersensitivity t cefixime or other cephalosporins, to penicillins or any component of the product

Headline: Single-dose Cefixime up to 2 g Adverse Reactions
Warning Description: Adverse reactions in small numbers of healthy adult volunteers receiving single doses of up to 2 g of cefixime did not differ from the profile seen in patients treated at the recommended doses.

Headline: Gastric Lavage, No Specific Antidote, Dialysis Inefficiency
Warning Description: Gastric lavage may be indicated; there is no specific antidote. Cefixime is not removed in significant quantities from the circulation by hemodialysis or peritoneal dialysis.

Headline: Hypersensitivity
Warning Description: Do not use in patients with a known allergy to Nitrofurantoin.
